_schema.description: Static Hijri-calendar reference used by brain/playbook.py for the 'ramadan', 'pre_ramadan_window:N', 'post_eid_window:N', 'islamic_month_in:N' and related calendar triggers. Hardcoded so we don't take a dependency on a Hijri converter and so the ranges are auditable.

fields.ramadan_windows: Hijri month 9 (Ramadan) Gregorian start/end. Source: Saudi Supreme Court / Pakistan Ministry of Religious Affairs official announcements. Update yearly.
fields.eid_ul_fitr: Day after Ramadan ends. Marks 'post_eid_window:N' triggers.
fields.eid_ul_adha: 10th day of Hijri month 12 (Zil-Hajj). PSX is closed; market reopens with risk-on/off based on slaughter-economy demand and global cues.
fields.ashura: 10th day of Hijri month 1 (Muharram). Public holiday in Pakistan; the 5d window around Ashura historically shows elevated PSX volatility.
fields.high_vol_islamic_months_gregorian_starts: Gregorian start dates for Hijri months 2 (Safar), 11 (Zil-Qad), 12 (Zil-Hajj) — the months PSX research identifies as highest-volatility (see https://bereview.pk/journal/index.php/ber/article/view/179). Each entry is a {start, end, hijri_month_number} object.

_schema.research_basis: Islamic Calendar Anomalies on KSE-100: Ramadan = positive returns + lower volatility (multiple PSX studies). Safar / Zil-Qad / Zil-Hajj = elevated volatility (GARCH/ARCH evidence).
_schema.maintenance: Append the next year's row each January. Existing dates verified against the Saudi Umm al-Qura calendar and Pakistan's Federal Ministry of Religious Affairs announcements where available. Treated as approximate (+/- 1 day) since moon-sighting can shift the announced start; the 'pre_ramadan_window' / 'post_eid_window' triggers absorb the uncertainty by widening the window.
